issues.title: macOS App Digest — July 10, 2026
sourceSummary: Mac Power Users #854: Obscure (and Excellent) Mac Apps
Product Hunt Mac, productivity and AI launches
MacStories coverage and Shortcuts Playground materials
GitHub searches for macOS, SwiftUI, clipboard, menu bar and automation projects
Official app homepages for every selected app
Reddit searches across the requested Mac communities, which did not return usable indexed evidence for this run

apps.name: Cotypist
apps.description: A local Apple Silicon autocomplete layer that predicts the next words inside everyday Mac apps.
apps.bestFor: Writers, operators and prompt-heavy users who want AI assistance without leaving the text field.
apps.source: Mac Power Users #854 and official homepage

apps.name: Choclift
apps.description: Turns an iPhone or Vision Pro into a touch launcher for Mac apps, Shortcuts, websites and emoji.
apps.bestFor: People who like Stream Deck-style workflows but want to use devices already on the desk.
apps.source: Mac Power Users #854 and official homepage

apps.name: Menu Drop
apps.description: A Sindre Sorhus menu bar utility for AirDropping files, folders, links and clipboard text by dropping them on an icon.
apps.bestFor: Mac users who send things between Apple devices constantly and want AirDrop to be one gesture faster.
apps.source: Mac Power Users #854 and official homepage

apps.name: Glaze by Raycast
apps.description: A Mac-first AI app builder that turns plain-language requests into desktop apps that run locally on the Mac.
apps.bestFor: Raycast users and automation-minded teams who want tiny internal tools without starting a full coding project.
apps.source: Product Hunt, Raycast and official homepage

apps.name: MacNotch
apps.description: A configurable productivity hub that puts widgets, shortcuts and drag-and-drop actions around the MacBook notch.
apps.bestFor: Notch MacBook owners who want glanceable controls without opening another dashboard window.

apps.name: AgentPeek
apps.description: Shows live Claude Code, Codex, Cursor and other coding-agent sessions from the Mac notch or menu bar.
apps.bestFor: Developers running multiple local AI agents who need prompt approvals, token usage and dev servers at a glance.

apps.name: Shortcuts Playground
apps.description: Federico Viticci's Claude Code and Codex plugin for generating, validating, signing and remixing Apple Shortcuts.
apps.bestFor: Automation users who have ideas for Shortcuts but do not want to hand-build fragile action graphs from scratch.

apps.name: SmartClipboard
apps.description: An open-source SwiftUI clipboard manager with Gemini-powered semantic search and sequential multi-paste.
apps.bestFor: Users who want a native menu bar clipboard with AI search rather than another heavyweight productivity suite.

apps.name: Wispr Flow
apps.description: System-wide AI dictation that turns spoken thoughts into polished text across Mac apps.
apps.bestFor: People who draft messages, notes, prompts and documents faster by speaking than by typing.
apps.source: Mac Power Users #854 and official homepage
